parentQuestion: When a patient sticks out their tongue and it deviates to the right, which nerve is damaged?

scaffolds.q: Your tongue is a muscle that can move in many directions.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Your tongue is actually made of several muscles working together. They let you move food around, help you speak clearly, and stick your tongue out.

scaffolds.q: You can stick your tongue straight out of your mouth.
scaffolds.explain: Exactly! When both sides of your tongue push equally, it goes straight out. If one side is weak, the tongue will curve toward that weak side.

scaffolds.q: CN XII is the abbreviation for Cranial Nerve number ___.
scaffolds.explain: CN XII = Cranial Nerve Twelve! In Roman numerals, XII = 12. The hypoglossal nerve is the twelfth and last cranial nerve.

scaffolds.q: The word hypoglossal means under the tongue.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Hypo = under, glossal = tongue. CN XII runs under the tongue to control the tongue muscles.

scaffolds.q: If one side of a muscle is weak, movement will curve toward the weak side.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Think of rowing a boat - if one oar is weak, you turn toward that side. The strong side pushes harder, so movement curves toward the weak side.

scaffolds.q: The tongue has muscles on both its left and right sides.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Each side has its own muscles and its own nerve. Left CN XII controls left tongue muscles, right CN XII controls right tongue muscles.

scaffolds.q: When the tongue deviates to the right, the right side is the weak side.
scaffolds.explain: Right! The left side pushes harder (its healthy), so the tongue curves toward the weaker right side. The deviation points to the problem.

scaffolds.q: The hypoglossal nerve controls tongue movement.
scaffolds.explain: CN XII controls all intrinsic and most extrinsic tongue muscles. Its purely motor - no sensation, just movement.

scaffolds.q: Tongue deviation toward one side indicates nerve damage on that same side.
scaffolds.explain: When protruded, the tongue deviates toward the weak (damaged) side. The healthy side pushes, the damaged side cant push back.
